vol_name: A-n-T: Attention and Temperament in Infancy
vol_description:
This study examines the way attention may be linked to temperament and emotional behavior from the first years of life.  

Children ages 4-months to 24-months with a variety of temperaments will be included in the study.  Infants will view pictures of faces and a children's video in order to examine patterns of attention.  

We will use state-of-the-art eye-tracking equipment to assess where the infants are looking.  Infants will also play with a variety of objects and toys as a part of a standard assessment of temperament.  These data will will allow us to see how patterns of visual attention may be associated with social and emotional behavior over time.  Since this is a multi-step study, participating families will be paid for their time and effort.

-Final gaze corrections split by task
-Synced videos split by task (MET footage and room footage)
-Documentation of which videos do not have synced footage 
-Datavyu coding sheets

The Behavioral Foundations of Friendship Study (BFFs) developed from Dr. Alicia Vallorani's NIMH F31 Predoctoral Fellowship sponsored by Dr. Koraly Pérez-Edgar. Participants were emerging adulthood, platonic friend pairs who came to lab and engaged in a social interaction while mobile eye-tracking data were collected. Later, participants watched clips of the social interaction while fMRI data were collected.

Video data included here are for those friend pairs where both friends consented to video data sharing. Behavioral videos, coding sheets and mobile eye-tracking videos are included. fMRI data are not stored on Databrary and will be shared via openfMRI at a later date.
